rulebooks.section_content: Background: Comparing waste generated with amounts directed to recovery and disposal clarifies how the organization manages waste impacts; composition informs suitable recovery/disposal routes. Waste from the organization's own activities; if data allow, upstream/downstream value chain waste may be reported separately. Refs bibliography [1], [4], [10], [11].

306-3-a guidance: Describe composition using waste type (hazardous/non-hazardous), industry-relevant streams (e.g. tailings, e-waste, food waste), and materials (biomass, metals, non-metallic minerals, plastics, textiles). Appendix reporting template may apply.

306-3-b guidance: Explain differences between waste generated vs directed to recovery/disposal (evaporation, leaks, losses, physical/technical vs administrative issues). State if data are modeled, from contractor transfer notes, externally assured, or from waste data audits.

Background: Waste management hierarchy prioritizes prevention, then preparation for reuse, recycling, other recovery, to avoid final disposal. Appendix template applies. 306-4-a composition guidance parallels 306-3-a (types, streams, materials).

306-4-b-ii and 306-4-c-ii (recycling): May specify downcycling, upcycling, composting, anaerobic digestion. 306-4-b-iii and 306-4-c-iii (other recovery): May include repurposing, reclamation beyond reuse preparation. Ref [1].

306-4-d: Onsite = within physical boundary or administrative control of reporting organization; offsite = outside. Reporting quantities/types diverted onsite vs offsite shows depth of waste management understanding.

306-4-e: Explain weight differences between onsite and offsite diversion (e.g. lack of onsite recovery infrastructure). May describe sector practices, standards, or regulations mandating recovery operations. Data: modeled vs measured (transfer notes, verification, audits).

Recommendation 2.3 guidance: Prevention is top of hierarchy. 'Prevented waste' may be estimated from actions reported under Disclosure 306-2-a; reduction due only to lower production capacity is not prevention. May include prevention in upstream/downstream value chain.
Background: Disposal is least preferred (leachate, methane, air emissions). Appendix Table 3 format. 306-5-a composition guidance parallels 306-3-a / 306-4-a.
306-5-b-iv and 306-5-c-iv: Other disposal may include dumping, open burning, deep well injection, etc.
306-5-d: Same onsite/offsite definitions as 306-4-d; demonstrates understanding of how disposal is managed.

306-5-e: Explain onsite vs offsite disposal weight differences (e.g. local bans on landfilling certain wastes). Sector practices, standards, external regulations. Data: modeled vs direct measurement (transfer notes, verification, waste data audits).
